info.name: EduSecure Pack 11 - Security Review
info.description: Postman structure for the API-executable parts of Pack 11 security testing. Use this together with docs/pack-11/manual-security-testing-playbook.md for browser-only, deployment, and audit-database checks.

exec: var requestName = pm.info.requestName || '';
var requestMethod = pm.request.method || '';
var requestUrl = pm.variables.replaceIn(pm.request.url.toString());
var scenarioMatch = requestName.match(/^([A-Z]+-\d+)/);
pm.collectionVariables.set('latestRequestName', requestName);
pm.collectionVariables.set('latestRequestMethod', requestMethod);
pm.collectionVariables.set('latestRequestUrl', requestUrl);
pm.collectionVariables.set('latestScenarioId', scenarioMatch ? scenarioMatch[1] : '');
pm.collectionVariables.set('latestPolicyProbe', requestName.indexOf('Policy Probe') >= 0 ? 'true' : 'false');
pm.collectionVariables.set('latestStatusCode', String(pm.response.code));
pm.collectionVariables.set('latestResponseBody', pm.response.text());

item.name: 00 Persona & Seed Data
item.description: Create or log in the personas and seed reusable IDs for later folders.

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for admin login', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('Expected authenticated admin login response', function () { pm.expect(json.authStatus).to.eql('AUTHENTICATED'); });

request.description: Use an already-provisioned admin account. Postman should retain the auth cookie automatically.

exec: pm.test('Expected create-or-already-exists outcome for Lecturer A', function () { pm.expect([201, 409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
if (pm.response.code === 201) { var json = pm.response.json(); pm.test('Created Lecturer A has LECTURER role', function () { pm.expect(json.roles).to.include('LECTURER'); }); }

request.description: Expected success when run as admin. Safe to rerun only if you change the email value.

exec: pm.test('Expected create-or-already-exists outcome for Lecturer B', function () { pm.expect([201, 409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
if (pm.response.code === 201) { var json = pm.response.json(); pm.test('Created Lecturer B has LECTURER role', function () { pm.expect(json.roles).to.include('LECTURER'); }); }
pm.test('Expected create-or-already-exists outcome for Student A', function () { pm.expect([201, 409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
if (pm.response.code === 201) { var json = pm.response.json(); pm.test('Created Student A has STUDENT role', function () { pm.expect(json.roles).to.include('STUDENT'); }); }
pm.test('Expected create-or-already-exists outcome for Student B', function () { pm.expect([201, 409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
if (pm.response.code === 201) { var json = pm.response.json(); pm.test('Created Student B has STUDENT role', function () { pm.expect(json.roles).to.include('STUDENT'); }); }
pm.test('Expected create-or-already-exists outcome for MFA Student', function () { pm.expect([201, 409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
if (pm.response.code === 201) { var json = pm.response.json(); pm.test('Created MFA Student has STUDENT role', function () { pm.expect(json.roles).to.include('STUDENT'); }); }
var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for Lecturer A login', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('Expected authenticated Lecturer A login response', function () { pm.expect(json.authStatus).to.eql('AUTHENTICATED'); });
var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 201 Created for assignment creation', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(201); });
pm.test('Assignment response includes id and expected title', function () { pm.expect(json.id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(json.title).to.eql('Pack 11 Security Assignment'); });
pm.test('Assignment remains open after creation', function () { pm.expect(json.open).to.eql(true); });
if (json && json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('assignmentId', json.id); }

request.description: Actor: Lecturer A. Preconditions: Lecturer A is authenticated. Evidence value: seeds a reusable assignmentId and confirms the seeded assignment remains open for later submission and grading checks. Expected result: 201 with a new assignment id.

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for Student A login', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('Expected authenticated Student A login response', function () { pm.expect(json.authStatus).to.eql('AUTHENTICATED'); });
var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 201 Created for submission upload', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(201); });

pm.test('Submission response includes seeded assignment id and a stable security shape', function () { pm.expect(json.id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(json.assignmentId).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('assignmentId')); pm.expect(json.verificationStatus).to.eql('VERIFIED'); pm.expect(json.storedFileReference).to.eql(undefined); });

pm.test('Submission response includes integrity metadata but no empty values', function () { pm.expect(json.hashDigest).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(json.digitalSignature).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(json.signatureAlgorithm).to.eql('SHA256withECDSA'); });
if (json && json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('submissionId', json.id); }

request.description: Actor: Student A. Preconditions: assignmentId is populated and Student A is authenticated. Evidence value: seeds a reusable submissionId and confirms Pack 11 upload responses expose integrity fields while omitting storage internals. Expected result: 201 Created. Set {{studentAUploadPath}} to a real UTF-8 text file before running.

item.name: Login - Lecturer A (for grade and space seed)

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for Lecturer A seed login', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('Expected authenticated Lecturer A seed login response', function () { pm.expect(json.authStatus).to.eql('AUTHENTICATED'); });

item.name: Create Grade for Student A Submission

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 201 Created for grade creation', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(201); });

pm.test('Grade response includes seeded submission id and value', function () { pm.expect(json.id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(json.submissionId).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('submissionId')); pm.expect(json.value).to.eql(78); });
if (json && json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('gradeId', json.id); }

request.description: Actor: Lecturer A. Preconditions: Lecturer A is authenticated and submissionId refers to Student A's verified submission. Evidence value: seeds a reusable gradeId and proves the base lecturer grading flow before later negative and policy-probe requests. Expected result: 201 Created.

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 201 Created for space creation', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(201); });

pm.test('Space response includes id and seeded identifiers', function () { pm.expect(json.id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(json.name).to.eql('Pack 11 Security Space'); pm.expect(json.code).to.eql('PACK11-SPACE'); });
if (json && json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('spaceId', json.id); }

request.description: Actor: Lecturer A. Preconditions: Lecturer A is authenticated. Evidence value: seeds a reusable spaceId for the space-authorization folder and confirms the canonical owner-created space shape. Expected result: 201 Created.

request.description: Run only after clearing the Postman cookie jar for the API host.

item.name: AUTH-05 Lecturer tries to create lecturer

exec: pm.test('Expected 403 for lecturer creating lecturer', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(403); });
var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for MFA student login', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('Expected login to return either AUTHENTICATED or MFA_REQUIRED', function () { pm.expect(['AUTHENTICATED', 'MFA_REQUIRED']).to.include(json.authStatus); });

request.description: Before MFA is enabled this should fully authenticate. After enablement it should return MFA_REQUIRED and clear the cookie.

exec: pm.test('Expected MFA setup success or already-enabled conflict', function () { pm.expect([200, 409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });

if (pm.response.code === 200) { var json = pm.response.json(); pm.test('MFA setup returns TOTP enrollment material', function () { pm.expect(json.mfaMethod).to.eql('TOTP'); pm.expect(json.manualEntryKey).to.not.be.empty; pm.expect(json.otpauthUri).to.not.be.empty; }); }

request.description: Capture the returned manualEntryKey or otpauthUri and add it to an authenticator app.

exec: pm.test('Expected MFA enable success or already-enabled conflict', function () { pm.expect([200, 409]).to.include(pm.response.code); });

if (pm.response.code === 200) { var json = pm.response.json(); pm.test('MFA enable returns TOTP-enabled state and recovery codes', function () { pm.expect(json.mfaEnabled).to.eql(true); pm.expect(json.mfaMethod).to.eql('TOTP'); pm.expect(json.recoveryCodes).to.be.an('array').with.lengthOf(8); }); }
var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for MFA-required login', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('MFA-required login returns a challenge id', function () { pm.expect(json.challengeId).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; });
if (json && json.challengeId) { pm.collectionVariables.set('challengeId', json.challengeId); }
pm.test('Expected MFA_REQUIRED after MFA is enabled', function () { pm.expect(json.authStatus).to.eql('MFA_REQUIRED'); });
var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for MFA verify', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('Expected authenticated response after MFA verify', function () { pm.expect(json.authStatus).to.eql('AUTHENTICATED'); });
pm.test('MFA verify response shows MFA-enabled AMR context', function () { pm.expect(json.mfaEnabled).to.eql(true); pm.expect(json.amr).to.include('pwd'); pm.expect(json.amr).to.include('otp'); });
var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 410 for consumed challenge replay', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(410); });

pm.test('Consumed challenge replay returns the expected structured error', function () { pm.expect(json.message).to.eql('MFA challenge has expired or is no longer valid'); pm.expect(json.errors._global[0]).to.eql('MFA challenge has expired or is no longer valid'); });

item.name: AUTH-10 Invalid MFA verify (repeat to lock challenge)

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected invalid-code or lockout outcome', function () { pm.expect([401, 429]).to.include(pm.response.code); });

pm.test('MFA invalid/lockout response message matches the current path', function () { if (pm.response.code === 401) { pm.expect(json.message).to.eql('Invalid MFA verification code'); } else { pm.expect(json.message).to.eql('Too many MFA verification attempts'); } });

request.description: Run this repeatedly against a fresh challenge until the response changes from 401 to 429.

item.name: Login - MFA Student (authenticated session for disable checks)

request.description: If MFA is enabled, complete the verify step first so the cookie jar holds a valid authenticated session before disable checks.

item.name: AUTH-11 Disable MFA with wrong password

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 401 for wrong password during MFA disable', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(401); });
pm.test('Wrong-password disable response uses Invalid credentials', function () { pm.expect(json.message).to.eql('Invalid credentials'); });

item.name: AUTH-12 Disable MFA with wrong verification code

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 401 for wrong MFA code during disable', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(401); });
pm.test('Wrong-code disable response uses Invalid MFA verification code', function () { pm.expect(json.message).to.eql('Invalid MFA verification code'); });
pm.test('Expected 401 when no auth cookie is present', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(401); });

request.description: Clear the Postman cookie jar before running.

item.name: SUB-13 Authorized metadata omits storage internals

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for authorized submission metadata read', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });

pm.test('Submission metadata returns key identifiers and verification state', function () { pm.expect(json.id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('submissionId')); pm.expect(json.assignmentId).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('assignmentId')); pm.expect(json.verificationStatus).to.eql('VERIFIED'); });

pm.test('Submission metadata omits storage and wrapped-key internals', function () { pm.expect(json.storedFileReference).to.eql(undefined); pm.expect(json.storageEncryptionNonce).to.eql(undefined); pm.expect(json.wrappedContentEncryptionKey).to.eql(undefined); });

request.description: Actor: Lecturer A. Preconditions: Lecturer A owns the seeded assignment and submissionId is populated. Evidence value: deterministic confidentiality check proving the metadata response omits storage references and wrapped-key style fields while still exposing the expected verification state. Expected result: 200 OK.

formdata.description: In Postman, rename the selected file part to a traversal-like filename such as ../secret.txt before sending if your client does not preserve a path-like name from disk.

request.description: Expected result is 400 Bad Request for an invalid submission filename.

item.name: SPACE-10 Student member views space without roster

exec: var json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Expected 200 OK for student member space view', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.eql(200); });
pm.test('Expected memberCount=1 for the seeded member-only view', function () { pm.expect(json.memberCount).to.eql(1); });
pm.test('Expected canManage=false for student member', function () { pm.expect(json.canManage).to.eql(false); });
pm.test('Expected isMember=true for assigned student', function () { pm.expect(json.isMember).to.eql(true); });
pm.test('Expected roster disclosure to be withheld from the student member', function () { pm.expect(json.memberships).to.be.an('array').with.lengthOf(0); });

request.description: Successful result should still show canManage=false and withhold roster data for the student member.
